data.title: India's Niva Bupa Health drops on data leak claim
data.description:
** Niva Bupa Health Insurance slides as much as 4.8% to 74 rupees, lowest in nearly a month

** Heath insurer says it is investigating if any customer data was leaked after an anonymous person claimed to have access to such data

** Adds, "As a matter of urgency, we continue to conduct investigation(s) of data leak and implement measures to mitigate the risk"

** Stock last down 3.6%; drops ~4% since debut on November 14, 2024

data.title: GIH Unit Receives Regulatory Approval for Stake Acquisition
data.description:
Gulf Investment House (ADX:GIH), or GIH, subsidiary Bait Al-Amar Al-Khaleeji secured approval from the Competition Protection Agency for the economic concentration process related to acquiring a 54.84% stake in Energy House Holding.

The Abu Dhabi-listed financial services company said Sunday it is completing the contracting requirements, which are subject to the seller's approval.

data.title: Palestine Trade Gap Largest in 4 Months
data.description:
Palestine’s trade deficit widened to USD 434.4 million in December 2024, the largest since August and up from USD 326.6 million in the corresponding month of the previous year, as exports fell further and imports rose.

Exports plunged 27% year-over-year to USD 164.2 million, as growth in outbound shipments to Israel slowed to 35% from a 68% increase in November, accounting for 84% of total exports.

Shipments to other countries also continued to decline, down 2%, though less severe than the previous 12% drop.

Meanwhile, imports surged 31% to USD 598.6 million, driven by a 17% rise in purchases from Israel, which represented 55% of total imports, and a 55% increase in imports from other countries.

For the full year, the trade deficit narrowed to USD 4,817.9 million from USD 6,183.4 million the previous year.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.94% at 74,600, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.95% to 22,579 mirroring global peers, as concerns over slowing U.S. growth added to ongoing worries over the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.64, boosted by the drop in the dollar and U.S. Treasury yields after economic data fuelled worries over the U.S. growth outlook.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.60 rupees, with the yield down marginally at 6.7029%, tracking the fall in U.S. yields.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 basis points at 6.28%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 4 bps to 6.04%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.20%.

data.title: Zhujiang Brewery's Profit Jumps 30% in 2024; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery posted a net profit attributable to shareholders of 812.6 million yuan in 2024, up 30% from 623.5 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

Earnings per share climbed 32% year on year to 0.37 yuan from 0.28 yuan.

The Chinese brewery generated 5.73 billion yuan in operating income, up 6.6% from 5.38 billion yuan in the previous year.

The company's shares jumped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Deep Blue Aerospace's IPO Timing Will Depend on Product Development, Capital-Market Conditions, CEO Says
Deep Blue Aerospace Aims to Conduct Orbital Flight Test of Reusable Rocket Nebula-1 in June or July, CEO Says
China's Leading Private Rocket Company Has Eye on Listing in 2028 — Interview
data.description:
(04:33 GMT) *China's Deep Blue Aerospace Eyes Public Listing in 2028, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *Deep Blue Aerospace's IPO Timing Will Depend on Product Development, Capital-Market Conditions, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *Deep Blue Aerospace Aims to Conduct Orbital Flight Test of Reusable Rocket Nebula-1 in June or July, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *China Can Catch Up to SpaceX in 2030 in Optimistic Scenario, Deep Blue Aerospace CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) China's Leading Private Rocket Company Has Eye on Listing in 2028 — Interview

By Clarence Leong

Deep Blue Aerospace, one of China's leading rocket companies, said it expects to meet the conditions for an initial public offering as soon as 2028.

The chief executive of the Jiangsu-based company said in an interview with The Wall Street Journal that while it intends to go public in the future, the timing will depend on the company's product development and China's capital-market conditions.

"Reusable rockets are a disruptive technology that will have huge market potential in the future," said Huo Liang, CEO of Deep Blue Aerospace.

Founded in 2016, the company is developing rockets that will be key to China's pursuit of its own Starlink-like satellite internet network. Deep Blue Aerospace's latest valuation is 5 billion yuan, equivalent to about $690 million, according to the company. It raised close to $200 million in funds last year, it said.

Reusable rockets--pioneered by Elon Musk's SpaceX--can dramatically lower the cost of accessing space. Deep Blue Aerospace plans to conduct an orbital flight and recovery test of its Nebula-1 rocket around June or July this year, Huo said. The rocket is designed to carry up to a 2-ton payload to low-Earth orbit, according to the company's website.

Huo, who worked for one of China's major space contractors before founding Deep Blue Aerospace, said SpaceX is far ahead of competitors because it has achieved frequent and stable recovery of its rocket boosters. However, he said that in an optimistic scenario, China could catch up to SpaceX by 2030.

"The industry's development in China isn't linear, and it'll certainly accelerate in coming years," Huo said. "More and more of the key elements are falling in place, just like pieces of a puzzle."

Besides rocket launches, Deep Blue Aerospace aims to operate its first commercial suborbital flight in 2027. Huo appeared in a livestream last year to sell a pair of tickets to the inaugural flight, with each ticket going for 1 million yuan after a discount. The tickets were snapped up.

Huo said Beijing's emphasis on the commercial space sector has had a positive impact on fundraising. Last year, the government's work report mentioned commercial spaceflight as one of the "new growth engines" for the first time.

A handful of Chinese companies plan to test reusable rockets this year, including Beijing Tianbing Technology, which is targeting June for the maiden flight of Tianlong-3. Huo said he expects a few rocket models to be in commercial operation by next year.

Write to Clarence Leong at clarence.leong@wsj.com

data.title: China's Deep Blue Aerospace Eyes Public Listing in 2028, CEO Says
China Can Catch Up to SpaceX in 2030 in Optimistic Scenario, Deep Blue Aerospace CEO Says
Mayfield Childcare Appoints Daniel Stone as Chief Executive Officer
data.description:
Mayfield Childcare has appointed Daniel Stone as chief executive officer, effective April 8, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

Daniel is the ex-chief operating officer and ex-deputy CEO of TheirCare, the filing said.

Company shares were up 2% in recent Monday trade.

data.title: Energy Services Groups Saipem, Subsea7 Agree on Terms of Potential Merger
data.description:
Oilfield services provider Saipem (SPM.MI) and subsea engineering and construction company Subsea7 signed a memorandum of understanding for the key terms of a proposed merger.

The new entity, to be named Saipem7, will have a combined revenue of 20 billion euros and a combined backlog of 43 billion euros, according to a Sunday release.

Under the proposed deal, Subsea7 shareholders will receive 6.688 Saipem shares for each Subsea7 share held. Subsea7 will also make an extraordinary dividend payment of 450 million euros before the completion of the merger. The companies expect annual synergies of 300 million euros in the third year after completion.

The shareholders of both entities will own an equal 50% stake in the resulting company, which will be listed on the Milan and Oslo bourses. The deal is expected to be completed in the second half of 2026.

Saipem's controlling shareholders Eni (ENI.MI) and CDP Equity also signed a memorandum of understanding with Subsea7's largest shareholder, Siem Industries, regarding their support of the proposed deal. The shareholders will appoint the majority of the combined entity's board and serve as its core key shareholders, according to a separate same-day release.

data.title: India to cap investment in EV charging for tariff relief as Tesla entry looms, document shows
data.description:
Key points:
  • India to limit investment in charging infrastructure at 5% - document
  • Charging cap to focus investment in manufacturing - source
  • Government expected to finalise EV policy next month - source
  • Tesla picks showroom space in Mumbai, Delhi

India's EV policy, which offers import tax cuts for foreign automakers investing in the country, will restrict them from using funds spent on charging infrastructure for such relief, increasing their car manufacturing, a government document shows.

India last year announced a policy aimed at attracting Tesla to manufacture EVs in the country and let such foreign carmakers import cars at a 15% tariff, from around 100% now, but only if they invest at least $500 million for a factory.

But the policy will mandate that automakers can count only 5% of their total EV investment as coming from creation of charging infrastructure, even if they spend much more on the power network, according to government document detailing draft rules which is not public but was seen by Reuters.

The government's plan comes just as Tesla gets closer to entering India with imported cars, having finalised two locations for showrooms. The restriction could upset those automakers who may want to invest a bigger chunk of their planned India investments into creating charging networks, which remain far and few in India.

An industry source privy to discussions with the government said the call is being taken as New Delhi wants companies to prioritise manufacturing, and not just charging networks.

In India's nascent EV market, many buyers have shied away from making purchases due to lack of fast chargers.

"Expenditure incurred on charging infrastructure would be considered up to (a) maximum 5% of the committed investment," the 47-page draft document from January 2025 stated.

The government is holding consultations with carmakers and other stakeholders on the draft rules and will finalise them by next month, said a source with direct knowledge of the matter.

India's ministry of heavy industries, which is spearheading the new policy, did not respond to an email seeking comment.

Tesla in a job advert last week said it is also looking for a "charging developer" who would "develop and manage pipeline of new charging" sites, and select locations for deployment.

The EV giant's chief Elon Musk put on hold his manufacturing investment plans for India last year, amid falling electric car sales globally.

Tesla's immediate India plan is to import cars and sell them in India. Musk and U.S. President Donald Trump however have repeatedly said India's tariffs for cars are too high.

The new draft rules said companies which commit to India manufacturing will also need to meet a minimum turnover of $577 million by the end of the fourth year of operation, and $866 million by the fifth year, to be eligible for lower tariffs on up to 8,000 electric cars per year.

If they fail to do so, they will need to pay a penalty of between 1%-3% of the revenue shortfall.

Other foreign automakers like Hyundai , and Toyota Motor have shown interest in making EVs in India at their existing and new factories.

data.title: Guanghui Energy Subsidiary Gets Environmental Approval for Coal Mine
data.description:
Guanghui Energy subsidiary Yiwu Guanghui Mining obtained environmental approval from China's Ministry of Ecology and Environment for the Malang No. 1 coal mine in Xinjiang, China.

The approval was for the initial phase of the mine, targeting an annual output of 10 million tons, according to a Feb. 22 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Malang No. 1 has a planned area of 85.5 square kilometers and planned production of 15 million tons per year.

data.title: India Stocks Hover at Over 8-Month Low
data.description:
India's shares fell by 709 points, or 0.9%, to 74,595 in early deals on Monday, marking the fifth straight day of decline, tracking a fall in Asian peers, following a downbeat session on Wall Street on Friday.

Traders are concerned about weak US economic data amid worries of potential rising inflation due to the impact of Trump's tariffs.

The BSE Sensex is lingering at its lowest level since early June 2024, with almost all sectors trading in the red, led by tech, banking, financials, and realty.

Persistent foreign fund outflows continued to pressure the index as market participants may prefer safer assets.

Caution grew ahead of crucial data from the US, including the PCE price indices and the second estimate of GDP growth, alongside speeches by several Fed officials.

The Nifty 50 dropped 0.9% to below 22,600, with notable losses from HCL Tech (-2.6%), IndusInd (-2.0%), Tech Mahindra (-1.9%), Tata Consultancy Services (-1.6%), HDFC Bank (-1.6%), and ICICIBank (-1.4%).

data.title: Broadway Industrial Group Appoints Group CFO
data.description:
Broadway Industrial Group redesignated Tiong Chi Sieng from chief financial officer to group CFO, effective Feb. 21, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

He will be responsible for the finance and treasury function of the group, the filing added.

data.title: Ampol Constructive on Refining Margins Normalizing, CEO Says
Ampol CEO: Expects Refining Margins to Bounce Around Long-Term Average
Ampol CEO: Encouraged by Chinese Oil Product Exports Remaining Stable
Ampol Confident That Refining Margins Will Continue to Rebound — Interview
data.description:
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol Constructive on Refining Margins Normalizing, CEO Says
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol CEO: Expects Refining Margins to Bounce Around Long-Term Average
(04:30 GMT) *Ampol CEO: Encouraged by Chinese Oil Product Exports Remaining Stable
(04:31 GMT) *Ampol CEO: U.S. Tariffs on Canada, Mexico Would Likely Drive Up Product Cracks
(04:31 GMT) Ampol Confident That Refining Margins Will Continue to Rebound — Interview

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Ampol is confident that refining margins will continue to recover after hitting a trough in the third quarter of last year, helped by lower interest rates stimulating demand for fuel and Chinese product exports remaining stable.

In an interview, Ampol Chief Executive Matt Halliday said it is encouraging that China hasn't flooded the market with surplus oil products despite ongoing weakness in its economy led by a soft property sector. Meanwhile, supply elsewhere is tightening at a time when fuel demand continues to grow, he said.

"We're pretty constructive on margins normalizing," Halliday said. "We expect them to bounce around the long-term average."

The Australia-based oil refiner and marketer on Monday reported a 68% decline in its 2024 profit to 234.8 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$149.3 million, when measured on a replacement cost-of-sales basis, which strips out the impact of movements in oil prices by restating the cost of sales using the replacement cost of goods sold. Its statutory net profit dropped by 78%.

Much of the earnings decline stemmed from refining margins worsening in the second half of the year. Ampol's Lytton Refiner Margin averaged US$7.08 a barrel in 2024, down 45% on a year earlier. But it reached a trough of US$1.48 a barrel in the third quarter when Ampol carried out maintenance work at its Lytton plant in eastern Australia and markets were awash with oil products.

Ampol said the material deterioration in global refining margins and the weaker operational performance of the Lytton plant meant its second-half earnings before interest and tax were some A$500 million lower than the same period of 2023.

Still, Halliday signaled the outlook is improving. The Lytton Refiner Margin was US$6.31 a barrel in January, improving from December but below historical averages. During February, Singapore product cracks strengthened by another US$2 per barrel.

Refining margins have responded to steps taken by the U.S. in the waning days of the Biden administration to target Russia's so-called shadow fleet that transports illicit oil, Halliday said. The Trump administration hasn't dialed back on those sanctions.

The illicit tanker fleet sprang up to evade Western sanctions on Russian oil, allowing Moscow to move crude to buyers in India and China by flagging ships in countries such as Gabon.

"Tightening of sanctions made life harder for Chinese and Indian refiners and that started to reset supply chains as well," Halliday said.

Ampol acknowledged that speculation over trade policy and shifts in global political dynamics is stoking more uncertainty in markets, with the U.S. not yet removing the threat of 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico entirely. Still, it is too early to conclude what the impact of this uncertainty will be, the company said.

"What we know is that Mexico and Canada are really important supply chains into the U.S. refinery sector," Halliday said. Tariffs on those countries "would reset supply chains and drive up volatility and product cracks. That would be our expectation."

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Ampol CEO: U.S. Tariffs on Canada, Mexico Would Likely Drive Up Product Cracks
RailTel Corporation of India Bags New Order Worth INR3 Billion
data.description:
RailTel Corporation of India has received a new work order worth 2.88 billion Indian rupees from the East Central Railway, India, according to a Friday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract provision of Kavach, an indigenous train collision avoidance system, on a low-density railway track in 502.2 RKm of East Central Railway.

The contract is to be executed by Feb. 20, 2027.

Shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Malaysia's Consumer Inflation May Accelerate to 2.6% This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0422 GMT - Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - China's official manufacturing PMI likely indicated another contraction in February with a 49.8 reading, Moody's Analytics says in a note. "Even as production chugs along to fulfill existing orders, a further 10% tariff in the U.S. on goods from China should hit new orders," it says. China's official gauge of factory activity contracted to 49.1 in January from December's 50.1 as factories suspended operations days ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday. As for the non-manufacturing PMI, retail sales should get a boost from Lunar New Year activity but construction will likely stay in the doldrums, it says. China's manufacturing PMI data is due Saturday. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0338 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session, driven by risk-on sentiment fueled by gains in U.S. stock-index futures and projected results from Germany's election. Initial estimates showed the CDU and its CSU sister party securing just under 29% of the vote, followed by Alternative for Germany, or AfD, with around 20%. Given that the election outcome aligns with expectations, analysts at Maybank say there are hopes of reduced political uncertainty. Additionally, signs of fading U.S. exceptionalism have emerged, supported by recent economic data. USD/SGD is 0.3% lower at 1.3319. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0222 GMT - More upside for JGB yields and the yen looks likely, Capital Economics' John Higgins says in a commentary. Japanese inflation and PMI data released Friday bolster Capital Economics' view that the JGB 10-year yield will continue to rise, the chief markets economist says. The ongoing rise in JGB yields probably stems from investors' reassessment of the Bank of Japan's monetary-policy outlook, Higgins says. The BOJ's policy rate is likely to reach 1.25% by end-2026, which feeds into Capital Economics' forecast for JGB 10-year yield to end 2025 at 1.75%. This could also buoy the yen against the dollar, says Capital Economics, which forecasts USD/JPY to reach 145 by end-2025. USD/JPY is 0.1% higher at 149.46. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Singapore's CPI likely rose 1.95% on year in January, according to the median estimate of six economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. That would compare with December's 1.6% increase. Headline inflation is expected to pick up due to a higher contribution from accommodation costs and private road transport, Sanjay Mathur, chief economist for Southeast Asia and India at ANZ, says in a note. Core inflation is projected to have eased to 1.6% last month from December's 1.8% rise, according to five of the economists polled. Core inflation will likely continue declining, partly due to last year's higher base from the GST hike, ANZ says. The data are due Monday. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0123 GMT - Asian currencies strengthen against the U.S. dollar in early trade amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would decrease the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Also, "playing to the emerging theme of a loss of U.S. exceptionalism, other services PMIs around the globe showed improvements," the senior FX strategist says. The weak U.S. data led to overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril adds. USD/KRW falls 0.2% to 1,431.80; AUD/USD edges 0.1% higher to 0.6369. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0035 GMT - Euro rises against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digest the outcome of Germany's election. Exit polls and early vote-counting suggest the Christian Democratic Union won around 29% of votes and will probably lead a coalition government, CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team say in a research report. "However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," the team says. Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team adds. EUR/USD rises 0.3% to 1.0499; EUR/JPY gains 0.2% to 156.42. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls 0.6% to 2638.50 in early trade as semiconductor and steel stocks retreat. Foreign and institutional investors are net sellers, as Wall Street's Friday falls are weighing on sentiment. Memory-chip makers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix decline 1.4% and 3.3%, respectively. Chip-making equipment supplier Hanmi Semiconductor loses 4.6%. Steelmaker Dongguk Steel slides 3.1%, paring Friday's sharp gains fueled by Seoul's move to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,436.50 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 3.2 bps at 2.856%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)
